title: 7 Managing 2-Step Security Tokens
body_text:
7	Managing 2-Step Security Tokens
7.1	Overview of Managing 2-Step Security Tokens
This chapter describes how to assign and manage 2-step security tokens. For general information on 2-step security, see the 2-Step Security Part.
User Management Security Officers (Security Officers) assign and manage 2-step security tokens for Client Centre Users and other Security Officers.
7.2	Assigning a Hard 2-Step Security Token to a User
Security Officers assigning a hard token to a Client Centre User or another Security Officer must have physical access to the hard token. Only deliver the hard token to the user after you芒聙聶ve assigned it to the appropriate user.
Assign a hard token to a user as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Enable 2-Step Security
. See Figure 1.
Note:	The 芒聙聹Enable 2-Step Security芒聙聺 option displays if a 2-step security token isn芒聙聶t assigned to the user. If a 2-step security token is assigned to the user, 芒聙聹Edit 2-Step Security Settings芒聙聺 displays instead.
Figure 1: Select Enable 2-Step Security
Select
Enable user for 2-Step Security using hard token
. See Figure 2.
Note:	The User Settings section content varies depending on the user芒聙聶s 2-step security set-up. If the user is already assigned a 2-step security token, the option to assign a token doesn芒聙聶t display. To assign a new 2-step security token to the user, the existing token must first be removed. Only one 2-step security token can be assigned to a user at a time. See Section 7.5, Removing a 2-Step Security Token from a User.
Figure 2: Select Enable User for 2-Step Security Using Hard Token
Enter the first few digits of the hard token芒聙聶s serial number (located on the back of the hard token), then select the correct serial number from the drop-down menu. See Figure 3.
Figure 3: Select the 2-Step Security Token Serial Number
Enter the one-time password displayed on the hard token, then select
Submit
. See Figure 4.
Figure 4: Enter the One-time Password Displayed on the Hard Token
Review the details on the confirmation page, then select
Confirm
to proceed. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Deliver the hard token to the user.
7.3	Assigning a Soft 2-Step Security Token to a User
Assign a soft token to a user as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Enable 2-Step Security
. See Figure 5.
Note:	The 芒聙聹Enable 2-Step Security芒聙聺 option displays if a 2-step security token isn芒聙聶t assigned to the user. If a 2-step security token is already assigned to the user, 芒聙聹Edit 2-Step Security Settings芒聙聺 displays.
Figure 5: Select Enable 2-Step Security
Select
Enable User for 2-Step Security using soft token
, then select
Submit
. See Figure 6.
Note:	The User Settings section content varies depending on the user芒聙聶s 2-step security set-up. If the user is already assigned a 2-step security token, the option to assign a token doesn芒聙聶t display. To assign a new 2-step security token to the user, the existing token must first be removed. Only one 2-step security token can be assigned to a user at a time. See Section 7.5, Removing a 2-Step Security Token from a User.
Figure 6: Select Enable User for 2-Step Security Using Soft Token
Review the details on the confirmation page, then select
Confirm
to proceed. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
Advise the user to install and register the soft token on their mobile device within 14 calendar days.
Note:	To replace a soft token, the user must delete the old token from their authenticator app. Once a Security Officer assigns a new soft token, the user is prompted to register their new soft token the next time they log into an application or function that requires 2-step security. In the authenticator app, the user must add another token and may need to select 芒聙聹Scan a QR Code
芒聙聺
if not automatically prompted. The steps to add a new token vary depending on the authenticator app used.  See the 2-Step Security Part for more information.
7.4	Viewing a User芒聙聶s 2-Step Security Settings Summary
View a Client Centre User or another Security Officer芒聙聶s 2-step security settings summary as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Edit 2-Step Security Settings
. See Figure 7.
Figure 7: Selecting Edit 2-Step Security Settings
View the user芒聙聶s 2-step security summary. See Figure 8 and the following table for information on each of the fields.
Figure 8: Viewing the Summary of the User芒聙聶s 2-Step Security Settings
Status
Indicates if the user芒聙聶s 2-step security token is locked or enabled.
If the user is assigned a soft token that isn芒聙聶t activated, this field displays the date that the registration period expires.
Registered Verification Method
Indicates the user芒聙聶s token type (soft token or a hard token).
If the user is assigned a hard token, its serial number displays, followed by the date that the token was assigned. If the user is assigned a soft token, the date the token was registered displays.
Replace token by
If the user is assigned a hard token, the suggested replacement date for the hard token displays. This field doesn芒聙聶t display for soft tokens.
7.5	Removing a 2-Step Security Token from a User
Removal of a 2-step security token applies to any situation where a user no longer requires that token (e.g., user no longer needs access, or user has a new mobile device). If a token is being replaced, the existing token must first be deactivated, then a new token assigned.
Remove a 2-step security token from a Client Centre User or another Security Officer as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Edit 2-Step Security Settings
. See Figure 9.
Figure 9: Select Edit 2-Step Security Settings
Select
Deactivate user from 2-Step Security
. Enter a reason for deactivating the token, then select
Submit
. See Figure 10.
Figure 10: Removing a Security Token from a User Profile
Select
Confirm
when prompted. Alternatively, select
Edit
or
Cancel
as required.
7.6	Unlocking a 2-Step Security Token
If a user with a 2-step security token enters an incorrect one-time password three times, their token is temporarily locked for 30 minutes. If the user incorrectly enters the one-time password three more times, the user芒聙聶s security token must be unlocked by a Security Officer.
Unlock a Client Centre User or another Security Officer芒聙聶s 2-step security token as follows:
Open the user芒聙聶s profile as described in Section 6.3, Searching for a User.
Select
Edit 2-Step Security Settings
. See Figure 11.
Figure 11: Select Edit 2-Step Security Settings
Select
Unlock user from 2-Step Security
, then select
Submit
. See Figure 12.
Figure 12: Unlock a User芒聙聶s 2-Step Security Token
END
